task_completion.description: Verify that File Integrity Monitoring (FIM) is configured for the target directory with real-time monitoring enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Wazuh configuration file (ossec.conf) or the Wazuh UI configuration editor showing the `<directories>` tag containing '/var/ossec/data/financial_records' with the attribute `realtime="yes"` inside the `<syscheck>` section.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the custom correlation rule with the correct ID and alert level.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a custom rule definition (in local_rules.xml or the Wazuh UI rules editor) starting with `<rule id="100250" level="12"`.

task_completion.description: Verify the rule triggers on 5 deletions within a 45-second window based on the parent rule 553.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look within the rule 100250 definition for the attributes `frequency="5"` and `timeframe="45"` (usually in the `<rule>` tag), and the tag `<if_matched_sid>553</if_matched_sid>`.

task_completion.description: Verify the custom rule is mapped to the correct MITRE ATT&CK ID.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look within the rule 100250 definition for a `<mitre>` block containing `<id>T1485</id>`.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Wazuh manager configuration was applied/restarted to make the changes active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal command like `systemctl restart wazuh-manager`, `/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-control restart`, or a success notification/toast in the Wazuh web UI indicating the manager was restarted.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with actual Wazuh configuration files or the official Wazuh web UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that edits are happening in standard Wazuh paths (e.g., /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf, /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml) using a terminal editor, or within the authentic Wazuh dashboard interface, rather than a generic unconnected text file.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent wrote valid XML rather than just pasting the prompt text.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the rules and configuration additions use proper XML tags (e.g., `<rule>`, `<group>`, `<directories>`) and are not just plain text notes or raw prompt instructions pasted into the files.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not manually inject fake alerts to bypass the configuration task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify there are no commands echoing fake JSON or log entries directly into `/var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.log` or `alerts.json`.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Directory: /var/ossec/data/financial_records. Rule ID: 100250. Alert Level: 12. Frequency: 5. Timeframe: 45. Parent SID: 553 (Wazuh default for 'File deleted'). MITRE ID: T1485 (Data Destruction). The rule must be a frequency-based correlation rule, which in Wazuh typically requires 'frequency' and 'timeframe' attributes in the <rule> tag, and <if_matched_sid> to specify the parent rule.
